reasoning: Iteration 0 failed with critical correctness bug: parallel aggregation doesn't merge partial sums across threads, causing ~50% revenue underestimation. MUST fix this first. After correctness fix, performance is 12x slower than DuckDB (930ms vs 77ms), leaving massive optimization headroom. With 2 iterations remaining, aggressively select ALL complementary optimizations: zone maps (30-40% gain), hash table improvements (15-25%), aggregation key optimization (5-10%), join reordering (10-20%), and SIMD (20-30%). These are independent and can be applied simultaneously.
focus_areas: Fix parallel aggregation merge before Top-K selection (CRITICAL)
Implement zone map skipping for 50% I/O reduction on date predicates
Pre-size hash tables and optimize hash functions to eliminate rehashing overhead
Add AVX512-accelerated filtering and hash probing for CPU-bound operations
Optimize join order to reduce intermediate hash table sizes
strategy_notes: Correctness takes absolute priority. The critical fix (index 0) adds a merge phase after parallel lineitem scan to combine thread-local aggregation maps before Top-K selection. After fixing correctness, all 5 performance optimizations are complementary and non-conflicting: (1) Zone maps reduce I/O at storage layer, (2) Hash table pre-sizing and better hash functions reduce CPU overhead, (3) Aggregation key packing improves hash performance, (4) Join reordering reduces intermediate result sizes, (5) SIMD accelerates hot loops. These operate at different layers and can be implemented together. With 2 iterations remaining and 12x performance gap, aggressive multi-optimization approach is justified. Expected combined speedup: 3-5x after all changes.
notes: First iteration must prioritize correctness over performance. After the critical aggregation merge fix, validation should pass and we can measure true baseline performance. All selected performance optimizations are low-to-medium risk and have clear expected improvements. The Query Optimizer should implement them as a unified patch.
